summary: Simon Späti argues that declarative data stacks represent the next evolution in enterprise data engineering, combining open-source flexibility with integrated platform capabilities. He traces the journey from monolithic vendor solutions to fragmented modern data stacks, positioning declarative approaches as the synthesis that offers both freedom and cohesion. The core thesis is that YAML-based configuration can replace most custom coding, enabling teams to deliver production-ready platforms faster while maintaining governance. Using Starlake as his primary example, he demonstrates how declarative stacks cover the entire data lifecycle—extract, load, transform, orchestrate—through configuration rather than code. The approach promises reduced technical debt, better reproducibility, and democratized data engineering accessible to non-programmers.
key_points: Declarative data stacks synthesize the best of monolithic platforms and modular open-source tools, offering integration without vendor lock-in
YAML configuration replaces imperative coding, making data pipelines reproducible, version-controlled, and accessible to 'YAML engineers'
Unlike black-box orchestrators like Airflow, declarative stacks are 'data-aware' with column-level lineage and explicit dependencies
The approach scales with complexity—ROI increases as enterprise data landscapes grow more intricate
Local development with DuckDB while targeting production warehouses reduces costs and accelerates iteration cycles
money_quotes: Imagine building enterprise data infrastructure where you write 90% less code but deliver twice the value.
A declarative data stack is a set of tools and, precisely, its configs can be thought of as a single function such as run_stack(serve(transform(ingest))) that can recreate the entire data stack.
Unlike Airflow, where each task has no idea what is happening inside of it, it's a black box.
The return on investment in the platform grows with rising complexity.
Breaking free from black boxes doesn't mean abandoning the open data stack, but rather integrating best-in-class tools under a unified, configuration-driven approach.
The declarative approach offers a sustainable path forward that balances enterprise requirements with development agility.
key_insight: Declarative data stacks represent a paradigm shift where configuration replaces coding, enabling enterprises to manage growing data complexity while reducing technical debt and democratizing data engineering beyond traditional programmers.
